title: Staying and Calling - Reflexive Verbs
description: Learn to use reflexive verbs for staying and calling in various contexts.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: You're staying here, informal. How would you say that?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Te quedas aquí. Good. Now, how would you say 'You're not staying here'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No te quedas aquí. Good. To make it a question, 'Aren't you staying here?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿No te quedas aquí? Good. How would you say 'Why aren't you staying here?'

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: por qué no te quedas aquí

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Por qué no te quedas aquí. Very good. How do you say 'I'm going'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Voy. Good. How would you say 'I'm going to stay'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Voy a quedarme. Good. How do you say 'I'm going to stay until seven o'clock'?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: voy a quedarme hasta las siete

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Voy a quedarme hasta las siete. How do you say 'I want to stay'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero quedarme. Good. How would you ask 'Do you want to stay?' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Quieres quedarte? Good. What was 'I must' or 'I owe'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Debo. Good. How do you say 'We must stay'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Debemos quedarnos. Good. How do you say 'I have to stay'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Tengo que quedarme. How would you say 'You have to stay' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Tienes que quedarte. How do you say 'I want to know why you have to stay here'?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: quiero saber por qué tienes que quedarte aquí

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero saber por qué tienes que quedarte aquí. Good. What was 'I can'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Puedo. How do you say 'I can stay'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Puedo quedarme. How do you say 'I can't stay'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No puedo quedarme. How do you say 'I can't stay tonight'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No puedo quedarme esta noche. How would you say 'We can stay'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Podemos quedarnos. How do you say 'We can't stay tonight'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No podemos quedarnos esta noche. What was 'to think'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Pensar. How do you say 'We think'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Pensamos. How do you say 'You think' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Piensas. How do you say 'What do you think?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Qué piensas? Good. How would you say 'Do you plan on staying here tonight?' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Piensas quedarte aquí esta noche? Good. How would you say 'I have stayed'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Me he quedado. Good. How do you say 'We have stayed'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Nos hemos quedado. How do you say 'We haven't stayed'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No nos hemos quedado. How do you say 'You have stayed' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Te has quedado. Make it a question: 'Have you stayed?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Te has quedado? Good. How would you say 'You call yourself' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Te llamas. How do you ask 'How do you call yourself?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Cómo te llamas? Good. Now we understand it's 'How do you call yourself?'

outro.text: You've learned how to use reflexive verbs for staying and calling in Spanish. Practice these structures to build confidence.
Try creating your own sentences using 'quedarse' and 'llamarse'. How would you say 'I want to stay here' or 'How do you call yourself?'
These reflexive verbs are essential for everyday conversations. Keep practicing to master them.
